2603.19152.title: VEPO: Variable Entropy Policy Optimization for Low-Resource Language Foundation Models
2603.19152.paper_summary: Large language models frequently exhibit suboptimal performance on low resource languages, primarily due to inefficient subword segmentation and systemic training data imbalances. In this paper, we propose Variable Entropy Policy Optimization (VEPO), which leverages Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards to incorporate deterministic structural constraints into the policy alignment process. This framework ensures prescribed sequence length, robust format consistency, and rigorous linguistic well formedness, all enforced during training. Central to our approach is a variable entropy mechanism that enables the model to dynamically calibrate the equilibrium between literal fidelity and semantic naturalness by modulating the exploration exploitation manifold. By integrating entropy tempered advantage estimation with asymmetric clipping, VEPO sustains robust exploration while mitigating policy collapse. Empirical evaluations across 90 FLORES-200, COMET-22, chrF directions demonstrate that VEPO yields substantial improvements in both tokenization efficiency and translation quality, bridging the performance gap for underrepresented languages.
2603.19152.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在低資源語言上的表現經常不理想，主要是因為子詞分割效率低下和系統性訓練數據不平衡。在本文中，我們提出了變量熵政策優化（VEPO），該方法利用可驗證獎勵的強化學習將確定性結構約束納入政策對齊過程中。這一框架確保了規定的序列長度、穩健的格式一致性和嚴謹的語言結構良好性，這些都在訓練過程中得到強制執行。我們方法的核心是一種變量熵機制，使模型能夠通過調節探索與開採的多樣性，動態校準字面忠實性和語義自然性之間的平衡。通過將熵調節的優勢估計與非對稱剪裁相結合，VEPO 維持了穩健的探索，同時減少政策崩潰的風險。在 90 個 FLORES-200、COMET-22 和 chrF 方向的實證評估中，VEPO 在標記化效率和翻譯質量方面均顯著改善，縮小了低代表性語言的性能差距。
2603.19152.author: Chonghan Liu et.al.
2603.19152.authors: Chonghan Liu, Yimin Du, Qi An, Xin He, Cunqi Zhai, Fei Tan, Weijia Lin, Xiaochun Gong, Yongchao Deng, Shousheng Jia, Xiangzheng Zhang
